guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: One or two close-look/sketchbook surfaces improve alpha memory without adding a bigger editor or copy burden.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Harden close-look/sketchbook opening/closing state only if existing tests reveal regression risk.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Select one close-look or sketchbook carrier per need and write compact callouts tied to existing notes.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Improve the visual silhouette or stamp placement for the selected carrier(s), not the whole system.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Use selected close-look/sketchbook surfaces as optional route support context without making them mandatory.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

finding: Lane 2 already completed the selected close-look carrier work for root-curtain and shore-pine.
The lane-1 contract only asks for close-look/sketchbook state hardening if existing tests reveal regression risk.
Existing close-look, sketchbook, and focused runtime smoke proofs are green.
The current runtime seams already clear close-look payloads, inspect bubbles, and sketchbook open/slot state across relevant close, journal-exit, biome-switch, menu, and reset paths.
action: Promote ECO-20260420-scout-394 for packet 147 so lane 1 can continue.
non_goals: No runtime code, tests, selected carriers, content, station, save, route, map, UI, or geometry changes for lane 1 in packet 146.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "opens a close-look card|opens the surveyed-biome sketchbook"

selected_carriers.why_selected: Route-defining Root Hollow evidence carrier with an existing compact sketchbook memory note.
selected_carriers.existing_memory_note: Dim root shelter catching drips above the cave.
selected_carriers.suggested_sentence: Root tangles slow drips and make a dim shelter above the cave floor.

selected_carriers.why_selected: Route-defining Open To Shelter evidence carrier with an existing compact sketchbook memory note.
selected_carriers.existing_memory_note: Wind-shaped pine holding the sheltered edge.
selected_carriers.suggested_sentence: Wind-bent shore pines mark where open sand starts settling into cover.

implementation_scope: Add close-look payload seeds for only root-curtain and shore-pine.
Reuse existing entry names and spriteIds.
Extend close-look tests for support and compact payload content.
avoid: new entries, sketchbook slots, journal layout, comparison rules, route behavior, station surfaces, world-map cues, or biome geometry
adding fallen-giant-log in this pass; it remains a future old-wood memory candidate but is weaker for packet 146's cross-lane selected-carrier handoff
lane_2_scout_refinement.shared_file_note: src/engine/close-look.ts is outside packet 146's lane-2 preferred paths, but lane 2 owns close-look candidate expansion in .agents/lanes/lane-2.md and prior close-look passes have used this data-only allowlist seam.

scope_notes: Added close-look seeds only for the two selected route/place-memory carriers.
Kept fallen-giant-log out of this pass as a future old-wood memory candidate.
Did not change entries, sketchbook slots, comparison rules, route behavior, station surfaces, world-map cues, or biome geometry.
lane_2_main_result.browser_proof: Not added for this data-only lane-2 pass; lane 3 can use the selected carrier list for visual proof if needed.

confirmed: Exactly root-curtain and shore-pine were added to close-look support.
The two payloads are compact, visual-first, and tied to existing route/place memory notes.
No route, sketchbook, station, map, comparison, save, or geometry behavior changed in the scoped implementation diff.

finding: Lane 2 already added close-look cards for the selected route carriers root-curtain and shore-pine.
shore-pine is already the Open To Shelter pine-cover evidence carrier.
root-curtain is already the Root Hollow root-held evidence carrier.
Lane 4 should prove those cards are optional route context after the normal inspect claims evidence, not add a new route requirement.
main_scope: Extend the existing Open To Shelter runtime smoke flow so inspecting shore-pine claims pine-cover before any close-look action, exposes a close-look bubble, opens the shore-pine payload, closes back to play, and leaves route progress unchanged.

Extend the existing Root Hollow runtime smoke flow so inspecting root-curtain claims root-held before any close-look action, exposes a close-look bubble, opens the root-curtain payload, closes back to play, and leaves route progress unchanged.
Keep the pass behavior-neutral unless the focused proof reveals a real bug.
non_goals: No route gates requiring close-look or sketchbook.
No new station pages, support ids, route frameworks, save fields, world-map cues, geometry, or broad copy rewrites.
No lane-2 selected-carrier copy or close-look seed edits unless fixing a proven regression.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot"

confirmed: shore-pine still claims pine-cover on normal inspect before any close-look action.
root-curtain still claims root-held on normal inspect before any close-look action.
Opening and closing each selected close-look card leaves active route progress unchanged.
The scoped implementation is test-only in src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts.
No route logic, close-look seed, support id, station page, save field, world-map cue, geometry, or player-facing copy changed for this lane-4 pass.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot"

result: Extended the Open To Shelter runtime smoke flow so shore-pine claims pine-cover on normal inspect, exposes the selected close-look card, and keeps route progress unchanged after opening and closing the card.

Extended the Root Hollow runtime smoke flow so root-curtain claims root-held on normal inspect, exposes the selected close-look card, and keeps route progress unchanged after opening and closing the card.
Kept the pass behavior-neutral: no route logic, close-look seeds, support ids, station pages, save fields, world-map cues, geometry, or player-facing copy changed.
verification: npm test -- --run src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "Open To Shelter outing|forest expedition slot"

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.
